content.assessment: This proposal formalizes discretionary gatekeeping against segregation-generating expansion across all maintainer types, including church and private schools. This partially echoes a structural-reform sensibility by seeking comprehensive-system-wide reach rather than state-sector-only levers, closer to the systemic ambition of the Finnish reform, though it operates at the margin (blocking future expansion) rather than restructuring existing tracking age or provision. The Finnish lens would note that Finland's own comprehensive design initially achieved low between-school variance through universal structural coverage, not selective administrative vetoes over expansion; the more relevant lens insight is that even comprehensive systems experience segregation through subtler, harder-to-police channels (school choice, informal within-school grouping), suggesting that a purely preventive administrative tool, however cross-maintainer in scope, may not reach the
